name: Matthew Schaefer
badges: Ace; Engine Fleet of Foot Transition
profile_summary: An overwhelmingly skilled and dynamic play-driving talent with unmatched developmental runway; all the makings of a franchise-altering No. 1 defenceman in the NHL.

name: Michael Misa
profile_summary: A dynamic, play-driving dual-threat scoring centre with exceptional details, contact skills, and machine-like motor. One of the most well-rounded forwards to come out of the OHL in years.

name: Porter Martone
profile_summary: A special talent whose passing vision and tactical genius could put him up amongst the NHL’s elite playmakers – a unique combination of hard and soft skills with top-line upside.

name: James Hagens
profile_summary: Dynamic skating, handling, and a full scoring arsenal could make the former No. 1 overall favourite a steal — if he returns to his play-driving ways.

name: Roger McQueen
profile_summary: A power centre with highlight-reel handling skill, a sniper’s release, playmaking ability, and defensive impact, but repeated injuries make him the draft’s biggest risk.

name: Jackson Smith
profile_summary: A dynamic offensive creator, explosive four-way mover, physical shutdown presence, and more all in the same skill set. All roads lead to the NHL, but which pathway will he take?

name: Caleb Desnoyers
profile_summary: A detailed 200-foot centre with a hard-nosed net-front game, deceptive open-ice playmaking, and flashes of a power forward game. More pace is required.

name: Carter Bear
badges: Finisher; Got That Bear; Vision Clinical; in Him
profile_summary: Perhaps the most intense competitor in the draft, he’s fearless, physical, and always making something happen — and he’s one of the top playmakers available, too.

name: Victor Eklund
badges: Got That Dog; In Him; Vision Fearless
profile_summary: A highly translatable and skilled winger with one of the best motors in the draft and a physical game that supersedes his size; a strong bet to become a top-six NHL contributor.

name: Jake O’Brien
profile_summary: A deceptive and smooth playmaker with high-end vision, a detailed approach, and lots of physical runway left. Improved pace could make him a top-six centre.

name: Justin Carbonneau
profile_summary: A highlight reel creator with top-of-class tools and the mindset to be a difference-maker. Decision-making needs work, but the top- six upside is clear.

name: Brady Martin
profile_summary: A one-of-a-kind manipulative playmaker who overwhelms opponents with monster forechecking ability and bone-crushing hits. Top-six NHL outcome hinges on skating development.

name: Anton Frondell
shades_of: Bo Horvat, Anders Lee, Brock Nelson
profile_summary: An overpowering two-way forward with a high-end release and off-puck instincts around the slot; upside hinges on play-driving and playmaking skill.

name: Lynden Lakovic
profile_summary: A fluid mover with dual-threat scoring and masterful control of the game. Few prospects evolve as much he has — physicality is the next step.

name: Radim Mrtka
profile_summary: A towering minute-eating defenceman on track to become an NHLer. He has top-four skill, sense, and pace some nights, but he’ll have to find them every night to maintain effectiveness.

name: Braeden Cootes
badges: Fearless Brain; Got That Dog; in Him
profile_summary: This one man forechecking machine already looks like an NHLer. With flashes of dynamic play-driving, there’s top-six potential, too.

name: Logan Hensler
profile_summary: A toolsy play-killer who steadily improved through the season. An easy projection to the NHL, and there’s more offence to give.

name: Kashawn Aitcheson
profile_summary: A punishing two-way defender with clutch scoring ability, play- killing potential, and elite physical skills. A future fan favourite in the NHL.

name: Cameron Reid
badges: Ace; Fleet of Foot Play Killer Transition
profile_summary: A play-driving defender with all-around skills and flashes of high-end offence, but he will have to tap into his offence consistently to fill a top-end role.

name: Benjamin Kindel
profile_summary: Relentless pace, vision, mid-range scoring, and defensive details define this hard-working forward’s game.

name: Malcolm Spence
badges: Clinical; Finisher; Versatility Got That Dog; in Him
profile_summary: A highly disruptive winger with intelligence, high-end details, and pro-style habits that will translate easily to the NHL, but lacks a dynamic element.

name: Cameron Schmidt
badges: Sniper Fleet of Foot Got That Dog; in Him
profile_summary: The fastest skater in the draft, a high-end shooter, electrifying rusher, and surprisingly physical — he has the special to make the NHL, but can he adapt?

name: Blake Fiddler
profile_summary: One of the toolsiest defencemen in the draft class with plus-level mobility and flashes of exciting puck skills, but shutting down the opposition will be his calling card.

name: Joshua Ravensbergen
profile_summary: Big, composed, and confident goalie who uses all of his tools effectively. Elite puck tracker that is able to come up with big saves.

name: Cole Reschny
profile_summary: Equal parts clever open-ice playmaker and robust down-low creator, he has the skill to fill the net — and the details to make it without dynamic skating.

name: Václav Nestrašil
badges: Fearless Raw; Got That Dog; in Him
profile_summary: A hard-working winger with creativity, handling, flashes of high-end thinking, and an increasingly violent side. A long-term prospect, but one with unique potential.

name: Shane Vansaghi
profile_summary: The ultimate bottom-six checking forward: Skill, intelligence, relentless pace, and overpowering physicality.

name: Bill Zonnon
badges: Fearless Vision; Got That Dog; in Him
profile_summary: A playoff-ready forward with special checking skills: Skill, intelligence, relentless pace, and playmaking.

name: Jack Murtagh
profile_summary: A versatile spark plug winger who took major steps this year as a playmaker, complementing his potent shot and high intensity. A high-likelihood middle-six winger.

name: Cullen Potter
badges: Breaker; Fleet of Foot Evasive Ankle
profile_summary: A high-end skater who brings a sniper’s release and some playmaking, but it was his growth defensively and physically that impressed this season.

name: Max Pšenička
badges: Vision Play Killer Got That Dog; in Him
profile_summary: Perhaps the draft’s biggest second-half riser, this high-pace breakout wizard adapted to the WHL instantly, and still has to more to give offensively.

name: Jacob Rombach
profile_summary: The modern shutdown NHL defenceman: Towering size, polished habits, and standout retrieval ability. Skill development in progress, but the want is there.

name: Kurban Limatov
profile_summary: A big, mobile defenceman that plays aggressively on both sides of the puck to impose himself every game.

name: Ryker Lee
profile_summary: A tremendously skilled winger who slices open defences with game-breaking handling and punishes them with his playmaking and heavy one-timer. Everything hinges on skating.

name: William Moore
profile_summary: A skillful forward with a quick processor, hard shot, fluid hands, and an ever-improving physical game — middle-six potential, but lots of development remains.

name: Nathan Behm
profile_summary: An inside-driven winger with game-breaking game and a knack for dominating entire shifts with a lot of runway for growth.

name: Sascha Boumedienne
profile_summary: An incisive breakout passer who grew in confidence and details throughout the season. Now, he looks capable of filling a two- way role in the NHL.

name: Henry Brzustewicz
profile_summary: A high-activity blueliner with a well-rounded toolkit who looks to create on every puck touch, particularly off the rush, but details need refining.

name: Kristian Epperson
profile_summary: A fearless, dynamic dual-threat creator with the physicality and intelligence to thrive alongside skilled linemates; the draft’s best re-entry candidate.

name: Luca Romano
profile_summary: A toolsy and detailed do-it-all centre who continued to flash high-end skill as the season progressed, but will need to increase the frequency and efficiency of his play-creation.

name: Benjamin Kevan
badges: Ankle; Breaker; Fleet of Foot Vision
profile_summary: With one of the draft’s best highlight reels, it’s clear he has the skill, vision, and pace to score in the NHL. But the details and physical game must catch up.

name: Alexander Zharovsky
profile_summary: A winger with elite hands and a unique development path makes for a real boom-or-bust type of NHL Draft pick.

name: Hayden Paupanekis
profile_summary: A tough, physical centre with puck skills, mobility, and defensive upside. A little bit more consistency required, but he’s poised for a breakout season.

name: Matthew Gard
profile_summary: A toolsy, defence-first centre with a cerebral style on track for a checking line role in the NHL. Flashes of playmaking and in- motion shooting suggest there’s more upside here, too.

name: Milton Gästrin
profile_summary: A well-rounded and intelligent two-way centre with strong supporting habits and enough offensive flair as a playmaker to have a chance of becoming a third-line centre in the NHL.

name: Mason West
profile_summary: The top high school prospect in the draft, his 6-foot-6 frame, mobility, and toolsy profile are punctuated by flashes of higher- end skill; an unlimited projection.

name: David Bedkowski
profile_summary: Arguably the most violent player in the class, with the mobility, reach, defensive details, and bloodlust to develop into a tremendous play killer.

name: Jack Nesbitt
profile_summary: An intelligent two-way centre with size, off-puck details, and flashes of high-end skill, but must up the pace and develop more contact skills.

name: Haoxi Wang
profile_summary: An enormously gifted skater with massive play-killing potential and flashes of vision. The biggest development project in the draft, but unique potential.

name: Ethan Czata
profile_summary: A detailed and extremely physical centre with a finishing touch, but upping the pace will be critical to increasing his three-zone impact.

name: Cole McKinney
profile_summary: A trustworthy two-way bottom-six centre projection who outworks almost anyone. He’s a capable play-connector and offers a physical net-front presence with scoring threat.

name: Ivan Ryabkin
profile_summary: A violent centre who can thread a needle and hammer a puck, he could turn into the ideal third-line pivot so long as his motor and consistent involvement take a step.

name: Jakob Ihs-Wozniak
profile_summary: A lanky winger with legitimate dual-threat potential as a shooter and as a distributor; projects as more of a complementary piece due to his lack of pace and limited play-driving.

name: Charlie Trethewey
profile_summary: An excellent skater with physicality, a big shot, and shutdown potential. Puck-moving and reads improved through the season, but more work remains.

name: Daniil Prokhorov
profile_summary: A missile on skates, he’s a violent forward who looks to throw crunching hits, fight to the net, and occasionally snipes off the rush. Sense is the concern.

name: William Horcoff
profile_summary: A rare midseason jump from the NTDP to the NCAA proved to be the right call for this towering, pass-first forward with defensive upside. Skating is the question.

name: Theo Stockselius
profile_summary: A big-bodied forward whose intelligence and skill led to elite production in the Swedish junior ranks; needs to improve his skating and pace to make the NHL.

name: Peyton Kettles
profile_summary: A shutdown presence with a condor-like wing span and flashes of skill. Lots of physical development ahead, but he’s one of the draft’s top shutdown options.

name: Eric Nilson
badges: Fleet of Foot Motor Play Killer
profile_summary: A fluid-skating two-way centre who drives play in transition and uses his motor as a defensive weapon; can he make his style work in the NHL at his size?

name: Tyler Hopkins
profile_summary: An intelligent, advanced support player who can execute plays at high speeds and play a variety of roles. Flashes of manipulation suggest more offence and skill to be unlocked.

name: Carter Amico
profile_summary: A toolsy right-shot defenceman who started the year as a potential first-round pick with his shutdown game, but reads under pressure and injury history are concerns.

name: Eddie Genborg
profile_summary: A physical two-way forward with a strong forechecking presence and good finishing instincts around the net. A relatively high floor, but skill could improve.

name: Malte Vass
profile_summary: A defensive defenceman who kills plays through his physicality and motor; also flashes ability in transition, but needs to refine his puck-moving.

name: Alexei Medvedev
profile_summary: Smart, athletic goalie that uses his skating and explosiveness as a strength. Not small, but makes himself larger with his positioning.

name: Sean Barnhill
profile_summary: A smooth-skating defenceman who towers over everyone, he excels at stifling rushes and killing plays, and wows every now and then.

name: Brandon Gorzynski
profile_summary: Speed, physicality, and a non-stop motor could make him a menace in the playoffs — and there’s some scoring upside, too.

name: Mace’o Phillips
profile_summary: A huge blueline presence with crushing hits, he brings a unique level of physicality. Continued growth in details and skill is required.

name: Everett Baldwin
profile_summary: Dynamic tools, confidence, aggressive puck rushing, and near- constant advantage creation make him one of the premier prep offensive defenders.

name: Cooper Simpson
profile_summary: His diverse release, dynamic handles, and heightened pace led to 49 goals this season, the most in the Minnesota high school circuit. Equally dangerous at the USHL level.

name: Edison Engle
profile_summary: A terrific skater with a game built off modern principles, he efficiently moves pucks, creates offence, and diffuses rushes. Next up: Another layer of physicality and skill.

name: L.J. Mooney
profile_summary: A tiny yet physical winger with dynamic skating and handling, he has the tools to play in the NHL at his size — but he must develop his reads and playmaking further.

name: Semyon Frolov75
profile_summary: Strong puck-tracking goalie and an elite athlete that makes up for some technical flaws with his other strengths.

name: Filip Ekberg
profile_summary: A high-IQ dual-threat scorer with a strong work rate who exploded offensively after a late-season top-line promotion in the OHL and enjoyed a record-setting U18s.

name: David Lewandowski
profile_summary: A dominant puck protector who throws reverse hits and bulldozes contact. Skating is a concern, but he flashes open-ice scoring ability and power forward elements.

name: Pyotr Andreyanov79
profile_summary: Athletic, aggressive goalie that consistently finds his game in big moments.

name: Viktor Klingsell
profile_summary: An undersized yet highly skilled offensive winger whose vision is among the best in the draft; NHL upside could be limited due to lack of physicality and pace.

name: Owen Griffin
profile_summary: An undersized defensive centre who flew under the radar until the playoffs, where he unlocked clutch goalscoring ability through a combination of hard skill and smarts.

name: Brady Peddle
profile_summary: A good mover who angles rushers to the outside, protects the middle at all costs, and kills plays efficiently, layering his stick and body. Upside is the question.

name: Viggo Nordlund
profile_summary: A diminutive yet highly skilled winger with dynamic offensive abilities; one of the more exciting boom-or-bust prospects in the draft.

name: Lirim Amidovski
profile_summary: A missile on skates, he impacts the game through non-stop movement, speed, and physicality. Though he has the ideas, the skill can’t keep up — yet.

name: Teddy Mutryn
profile_summary: A physical winger with a heavy release and playmaking flashes, he looks like a future bottom-six winger who steps it up in the playoffs — provided the skill takes a step.

name: Shamar Moses
profile_summary: A deceptive, physical winger with and impressive flashes of puck skills, but future upside hinges on further skating and pace development.

name: Lucas Beckman
profile_summary: Strong-skating goalie who uses his edges well to remain in position and track plays. A bit of calm added to his game will go a long way.

name: Jimmy Lombardi
profile_summary: A high-skill centre with play-driving potential and defensive details, but must up the pace and improve creation frequency to make it.

name: Quinn Beauchesne
profile_summary: A high-end skater and active defender with shutdown potential and quality tools, but handling mistakes and turnovers under pressure can limit his effectiveness.

name: Alex Huang
profile_summary: An incredibly mobile defenceman who defends the middle and creates from the point. Raw, but his tools are worth betting on.

name: Liam Pettersson
profile_summary: A lanky puck-moving defenceman with strong transition results and a projectable set of feet. Possesses intriguing upside, but remains raw and needs to tighten things up defensively.

name: Bryce Pickford
profile_summary: Detailed defenceman turned goalscoring phenom, he’s one of the draft’s feel-good stories. He has the shot, but can he develop the rest?

name: Anthony Allain-Samaké
profile_summary: A strong skater and frequent activator, the points didn’t come close to representing his offensive threat. A boom candidate who could become a bottom-pair guy built for the modern game.

name: Noah Read
profile_summary: An intelligent, detailed, and high-speed transition player who didn’t get many opportunities to showcase his puck skill, but has huge breakout potential in a larger role next season.

name: Mads Kongsbak Klyvø
profile_summary: A toolsy, above-average-skating two-way winger who shows finesse in transition; could have more untapped offensive upside based on his late-season performances.

name: Maxim Agafonov
profile_summary: A right-shot that can be relied upon to transport the puck up the ice and fill any role capably.

name: Petteri Rimpinen
profile_summary: A World Juniors star who was passed over last year; posted some of the strongest results among all Liiga starters this season

name: Owen Martin
profile_summary: This late-season riser combines a detailed defensive game with transition and playmaking skill. If the tools takes a step, he could surprise.

name: Adam Benák
profile_summary: With unmatched work rate, precision passing, and a lengthy résumé, he’s one of the draft’s most well-known players. A workhorse, but will have to find another level.

name: Harry Nansi
profile_summary: A slick playmaker who pushes the pace of play and blends physicality with high-end handling flashes. An extremely raw, but exciting project.

name: Carlos Händel
profile_summary: A smooth-skating blueliner whose composure on-puck makes him an asset in transition. Lots of defensive growth this season, but needs to add more pace and urgency.

name: Evan Passmore
profile_summary: A highly aggressive rush defender who leverages his size, physicality, and mobility to get early stops in transition. A potential breakout candidate with more minutes and a larger role.

name: Max Westergård
profile_summary: A productive two-way winger whose offence flows through his off-puck instincts and ability to anticipate; had a promising late- season stint in the SHL where he showed more pace.

name: Jack Ivankovic
profile_summary: A game-stealing, undersized goalie with a lengthy international résumé, he could significantly outperform his draft position – with a bit of growth.

name: Lasse Boelius
profile_summary: A mobile and quick defenceman who led the way for Finland offensively during international play; vision and two-way output need to improve.

name: Kieren Dervin
profile_summary: His NHL-ready frame, straight-line driving, and supporting qualities only make up part of his mature game. A dedicated two- way centre profile who easily meshes with others.

name: Ryan Miller
profile_summary: An industrious, direct forward with a highly nuanced physical game and flashes of high-end playmaking.

name: Vojtěch Čihař
profile_summary: A relentless winger with speed and a tenacious style. He gets to the net and creates turnovers, but upside might be limited.

name: Aidan Park
profile_summary: A clever centre with a never-say-die motor and polished defensive habits, Park has the makings of a bottom-six centre if he improves his skating and handling at speed.

name: Kirill Yemelyanov
profile_summary: A reliable centre who possesses a strong motor and advanced defensive habits making him an easy bottom-six projection.

name: Noah Laberge
profile_summary: An intelligent and mobile blueliner who specializes in suppressing opposing rushes. Has the foundation to become a bottom-pair defender if the physical tools improve.

name: Theodor Hallquisth
profile_summary: A puck-moving defenceman who generates offence through his activations and possesses a projectable release; needs to improve his skating to realistically make it in the NHL.

name: Daniel Nieminen
profile_summary: A fabulous skater who earned a big role in Liiga as a 18-year-old after getting passed on last year; could be due for an even bigger breakout next year.

name: Roman Bausov
profile_summary: A lanky, right-shot defenceman who is a certified play killer already, but will need to fill out his frame.

name: Tommy Lafrenière
profile_summary: A detailed, net-driving multi-position forward with an endless motor. Skill is the question, but he takes no shifts off.

name: Nathan Quinn
profile_summary: A highly cerebral forward who excels in small-ice scenarios. The physical tools will need to come, but if they do, he’ll be a versatile third-line centre.

name: Karl Annborn
profile_summary: A well-rounded, mature defenceman who contributes on both ends of the rink. His jack-of-all-trades status could give him bottom-pairing upside in the NHL.

name: Mateo Nobert
profile_summary: An intelligent forward who excels at short-range passing. Better physical skills would compensate for his lack of size and speed, and could make him a bottom-six centre.

name: Bruno Idžan
profile_summary: A midseason move from Croatia to the USHL changed this re- entry’s trajectory. A credible off-puck scorer with flashes of advanced checking skills.

name: Tomáš Poletín
profile_summary: A versatile two-way winger with projectable habits and bottom- six NHL upside; played his best hockey of the season in international competition.

name: Jordan Charron
profile_summary: A highly energetic, physical, and strong-skating winger who pushes the pace of play and flashes dynamic elements, but generating chances consistently is the next step.

name: Nikita Tyurin
profile_summary: A puck-moving defender who has shown tool improvements all year to earn an important role on a championship contender.

name: Gustav Hillström
profile_summary: A big-bodied forward with a projectable motor, decent feet, and intriguing physical game. The lack of higher-end offensive skill limit the upside.

name: Matouš Kucharčík
profile_summary: A tall, mobile forward with flashy puck skills and defensive intensity. Turning moments of dynamic ability into gamely staples could make him a breakout prospect.

name: David Rozsíval
profile_summary: A hard-working puck-protecting winger with a knack for keeping the cycle going. Brings some two-way value, but skill might be limiting.

name: Benjamin Rautiainen
profile_summary: A skilled, offensively-minded winger who had a notable breakout season in Liiga as an re-entry; a good showing at the World Juniors also helped to raise his draft stock.

name: Måns Goos
profile_summary: Raw potential in an NHL-sized body.

name: Love Härenstam
profile_summary: Swedish starter at U18s who is athletic.

name: Samuel Meloche
profile_summary: Strong QMJHL playoff goalie that handles pucks better than most.

name: Michal Pradel
profile_summary: One of the most impressive goalies in the second half of the season after moving to USHL.

name: Jacob Cloutier
profile_summary: A fearless workhorse who attacks with energy, physicality, and thrives in transition; turning his deceptive playmaking flashes into more consistent chances will be key.

name: Émile Guité
profile_summary: 2024’s QMJHL Rookie of the Year owns a phenomenal release and some impressive handling, but his reason marked a significant step back.

name: Will Reynolds
profile_summary: A straightforward shutdown defenceman who hits hard, blocks shots, and defends the house. Could develop into a third-pair insulator.

name: Ilyas Magomedsultanov
profile_summary: A physically assertive defenceman whose game revolves around his mobility and aggressiveness on defence.

name: Arvid Drott
profile_summary: An explosive winger with projectable straight-line speed and a direct, shoot-first mentality; produced well on a good team, but lacks higher-end offensive instincts and playmaking touch.

name: Felix Färhammar
profile_summary: An active two-way defenceman with puck-moving ability and a physical edge; lacks a true standout element, but his well- roundedness will serve him well in the professional ranks.

name: Linus Funck
profile_summary: A lanky defenceman who posted strong defensive results at the J20 level while also flashing upside as a playmaker; lacks pace and consistency as a puck-mover.

name: Zeb Lindgren
profile_summary: A mobile defenceman with projectable physical tools and intriguing puck-moving flashes; limited output concerns.

name: Melvin Novotny
profile_summary: A crafty, skilled, offensively-minded winger who generates chances through his smarts; skating needs to improve.

name: Asher Barnett
profile_summary: The NTDP captain uses controlled footwork and clever timing to force plays to the outside. A stabilizing presence, but will have to add a special element.

name: William Belle
profile_summary: A powerful winger with a strong motor, he could be a menace on the forecheck with improved timing and speed.

name: Conrad Fondrk
profile_summary: A skilled forward with an NHL-calibre shot, an arsenal of passing skills, and plenty of moves, but his physicality, pace, and inside play must improve.

name: Matthew Lansing
profile_summary: A fluid and mechanically consistent skater, he offers two-way engagement, off-puck support, and flashes of playmaking ability. Inconsistent skill could limit his upside.

name: Sam Laurila
profile_summary: A puck-mover with a play-killing streak, he tilted the ice in Fargo’s favour every time he stepped out this year. He’s among the youngest re-entries and USHL’s most-improved players.

name: Mason Moe
profile_summary: An adaptable, multi-position forward, he complements more skilled linemates with his details and improving physicality.

name: Ryan Rucinski
profile_summary: A net-front-scoring and draw-winning forward who punches above his weight class. Not dynamic, but could be dynamite in the NCAA.

name: Drew Schock
profile_summary: A smooth skater with great edges and strong agility, he has a standout NHL tool. The playmaking is improving, too. But the physicality will have to take a step.

name: Ashton Schultz
profile_summary: An intelligent two-way centre with impressive details. Skill comes and goes, but he could fill a bottom-six role with added intensity and playmaking.

name: Ethan Wyttenbach
profile_summary: An intelligent and adaptable two-way winger, he has NHL- calibre playmaking, details, and skills. It all hinges on skating and physicality growth.

name: Will Sharpe
profile_summary: A do-it-all defenceman with mobility and vision. The lack of a standout trait might limit the upside, but he flashes more.

name: Štěpán Hoch
profile_summary: A responsible Czech centre with details, energy, and the reach to steal pucks and intercept passes. Finding another gear as skater will be gear.

name: Maxim Schäfer
profile_summary: An aggressive forechecking winger who delivers checks along the boards and has a strong net-front presence. With some development as a skater and handler, he could be a depth option.

name: Ján Chovan
profile_summary: A big-bodied centre with a detail-oriented play style; played his best hockey of the season during international competition.

name: Trenten Bennett
profile_summary: The most imposing-looking goalie in the draft.

name: Alexis Cournoyer
profile_summary: Late-rising goalie with fun back story that uses size to his advantage.

name: Gabriel D’Aigle
profile_summary: Strong pedigree and history, controls rebounds well.

name: Burke Hood
profile_summary: Big battler in the crease that relies on athleticism.

name: Elijah Neuenschwander
profile_summary: If you ignore his national team games, he could be a fit for you.

name: Arseni Radkov
profile_summary: Wildcard amongst the goalie class, explosive and one of younger players.

name: Ivan Tkach-Tkachenko
profile_summary: A proven starter already at MHL level who can handle heavy workload.

name: Andrei Trofimov
profile_summary: Strong, technically-sound Russian goalie.

name: Grayden Robertson-Palmer
profile_summary: His relentless motor drives his game, made better by his physical profile, unrelenting pace and deft playmaking

name: Blake Vanek
profile_summary: Hulking frame, strength, and a goalscorer’s touch highlight his game. He’s more raw than refined, but the upside is palpable.

name: Kale Dach
profile_summary: The BCHL’s best distributor, his vision, supporting habits, and physical runway are a laundry list of positives surrounding his profile.

name: Charlie Cerrato
profile_summary: One of the NCAA’s top-scoring first-year players after a late- season tear — perhaps third’s time a charm for this NTDP alum.

name: Cole Chandler
profile_summary: Primarily a defence-first forward, but his flashes of higher-level playmaking and skill left us wondering about his upside.

name: Alexis Mathieu
profile_summary: A hard-nosed defenceman with a good skating foundation. Fairly limited on-puck, but could-become a third-pair shutdown defenceman with improved reads.

name: Mikhail Fyodorov
profile_summary: A winger with a strong shot that looks to use a mix of skating, handling, and pure determination to gain chances.

name: Artyom Gonchar
profile_summary: An interesting profile limited by size, he gained the trust of his team with his reliability on both ends of the puck.

name: Aron Dahlqvist
profile_summary: A tall defensive defenceman with a notable physical flair to his game; needs to improve his handling and puck-moving in transition to be able to play his game at the next level.

name: Mikkel Eriksen
profile_summary: A skilled, offensively-minded centre who creates in opportunistic ways and has dual-threat qualities; lack of size, physicality, and lower pace of play limit the NHL upside.

name: Tinus Luc Koblar
profile_summary: A big-bodied yet raw forward with good mechanical base and intriguing offensive flashes; could have more untapped potential, but there’s also a lot of uncertainty around the projection.

name: Jackson Crowder
profile_summary: A checking forward with physicality and flashes of skill, he could surprise as he develops his skating more.

name: Richard Gallant
profile_summary: A hard-working winger who was among the NTDP’s top scorers, he excels at give-and-go plays. Physicality and skating are the mains hurdle between him and the NHL.

name: Caeden Herrington
profile_summary: A comfortable activator and physical in-zone defender, he’s USHL’s top blueliners in his re-entry season. Skating is the question.

name: Jacob Kvasnicka
profile_summary: A pacey winger who supports skilled players effectively and flashes of playmaking, he could become a bottom-six piece with improved physicality and defensive stick work.

name: Aiden Foster
profile_summary: A glove-dropping, hard-hitting winger steadily learning how to leverage physicalty into offensive impact.

name: Carter Klippenstein
profile_summary: A hard-hitting power forward with flashes of vision and scoring sense, waiting to be unlocked by another layer of skill.

name: Luke Vlooswyk
profile_summary: A big defenceman with an edge and in-motion stopping ability, but puck skills must improve.

name: Reko Alanko
profile_summary: A towering yet very raw defenceman who flashed impressive poise with the puck towards the end of the season; an intriguing player to keep an eye in the upcoming years.

name: Atte Joki
profile_summary: A hard-working two-way centre who brings the fire on the forecheck; lack of higher-end offensive skill limits the upside.

name: Brent Solomon
profile_summary: Pinpoint shot placements and activations into high danger areas are the name of his game, filling nets across his entire high school career. Pace is the next step.

name: Jérémy Loranger
profile_summary: The BCHL’s top scorer and MVP; his motor and pace of play are unmatched in the BCHL. Undersized, but his vision, shooting, and handling skill could translate.

name: Gabe Chiarot
profile_summary: A tireless, unrelenting checker whose finishing ability hints at higher potential, but must leverage his puck skills more to improve his NHL odds.

name: Grant Spada
profile_summary: An assertive, physical blueliner who makes up for a lack of an offensive dimension with quality shutdown ability. Improving footspeed will be critical to increasing defensive efficiency.

name: Will Murphy
profile_summary: A mean, physical defenceman who loves to lay the body. With improved edgework, could become a third-pair crusher.

name: Yegor Borikov
profile_summary: With NHL-calibre skating and shooting, he could become effective depth — if the motor and forechecking improves.

name: Dmitri Isayev
profile_summary: A hard-working winger that has the checking game to grow into a professional player and looks to do his playmaking from the point.

name: Wilson Björck
profile_summary: A quick and versatile forward who had a big breakout year in the J20 ranks; showed more offensive ability than in the past, but also played in a very favourable environment.

name: Victor Hedin Raftheim
profile_summary: A lanky defenceman with projectable skating ability; a lot of rawness and uncertainty around the projection, but more offensive flashes than the production would suggest.

name: Nolan Roed
profile_summary: One of the USHL’s most productive players, but he does it through clever off-puck creation in lieu of dynamic skill.

name: Shea Busch
profile_summary: Joining Everett midseason, he enjoyed a breakout run in the WHL with his net-front skills and physical ability.

name: Brendan Dunphy
profile_summary: A rangy and active defenceman who thrives in battles and can surprise with his offensive touch in moments.
